Figurative Portrait with Truchet Tiles

Load packages:

library(ggplot2)
library(imager)
library(dplyr)
library(lwgeom)
library(MexBrewer)
library(purrr)
library(sf)
library(truchet)

Create a figurative mosaic

Read the image using imager::load.image():

jn <- load.image("poe.jpeg")

Image info:

jn
Image. Width: 407 pix Height: 407 pix Depth: 1 Colour channels: 3

This is the image:

plot(jn)

Resize image:

jn_rs <- imresize(jn, scale = 2/10, interpolation = 6)
jn_rs
Image. Width: 81 pix Height: 81 pix Depth: 1 Colour channels: 3

Convert to data frame:

df <- jn_rs %>%
  grayscale() %>% 
  as.data.frame()
df <- df %>% 
  # Reverse the y axis
  mutate(y = -(y - max(y)),
         # The modulus of x + y can be used to create a checkerboard pattern
         tiles = case_when((x + y) %% 2 == 0 ~ "Al",
                           (x + y) %% 2 == 1 ~ "Cl"),
         b = case_when((x + y) %% 2 == 0 ~ 1 - value,
                       (x + y) %% 2 == 1 ~ value))

Function st_truchet_fm() is used to create a mosaic of flexible tiles. Column b is scaled to avoid completely white or completely black tiles:

# Start a timer
start_time <- Sys.time()

# Assemble mosaic
mosaic <- st_truchet_fm(df = df %>% 
                          mutate(b = b * 0.9999))

# End timer
end_time <- Sys.time()

# Calculate time
end_time - start_time
Time difference of 30.88818 secs

Since the mosaic is a simple features data frame it can be plotted using geom_sf() in {ggplot2}:

mosaic %>%
  mutate(color = factor(color)) %>%
  ggplot() + 
  geom_sf(aes(fill = color),
          color = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("1" = "white", "2" = "black")) + 
  coord_sf(expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin=unit(c(0.125, 0.125, 0.125, 0.125), 'in'))



ggsave(filename = "poe.pdf",
       width = 8,
       height = 8,
       units = "in")
